Details: Ozone combined with hydrogen peroxide is shown to be effective for cyanide removal at low concentrations in water. One bench-scale study reported 90 percent removal for an initial cyanide concentration of 0.3 mg/L with an ozone dose of 3.0 mg/L and a hydrogen peroxide dose of 15 mg/L [1361]. Approximately 72 percent removal was reached for an initial cyanide dose of 0.5 mg/L by adding 15 mg/L hydrogen peroxide and 7.0 mg/L ozone [1361].The study suggested increased cyanide removal with increasing hydrogen peroxide doses (from 2 mg/L to 15 mg/L) with a fixed ozone dose.
